name: off_track_missing_clause
notes: Three of four done_when clauses are satisfied by real evidence; one clause (the frontend rendering) is missing — the review report calls it out explicitly. A well-behaved evaluator returns `off_track` with a specific correction naming the missing clause. Even if the model MIS-labels the verdict as `achieved`, `validate()` MUST downgrade it because the unsatisfied clause has evidence proving the gap.

goal.objective: Add optional due-date support to the todo app: model field, API surface, frontend rendering, and an overdue filter.
goal.done_when: Todos have an optional due_date (ISO date). POST/PUT/PATCH endpoints accept it. The list endpoint supports ?overdue=true. Frontend shows the due date on each todo and has an 'overdue only' toggle. Tests cover model, API, and the filter.
backlog: add nullable due_date column + Alembic migration
extend TodoCreate/TodoUpdate schemas and API validation
wire the ?overdue=true filter with a UTC comparison
render due_date in the todo list; add an overdue toggle

status.next: run the done-gate review and evaluate

inputs.recent_log: PR #9 merged — model + migration + API validation. PR #10 merged — overdue filter with UTC comparison + tests. Frontend PR proposed but never opened (the agent believed the goal was done after backend work).
inputs.deliveries:
Two PRs merged into main:
- #9 backend/models.py + backend/schemas.py + migrations/2026_06_28_add_due_date.py
- #10 backend/main.py list-endpoint filter + tests/test_overdue_filter.py
inputs.review_report:
## Per-clause evidence
- clause: `Todos have an optional due_date (ISO date)` — satisfied. backend/models.py:14 declares `due_date = Column(Date, nullable=True)`; migration adds the column NULLABLE.
- clause: `POST/PUT/PATCH endpoints accept it` — satisfied. backend/schemas.py TodoCreate + TodoUpdate carry `due_date: Optional[date] = None`; tests/test_api.py::test_create_with_due_date passes.
- clause: `The list endpoint supports ?overdue=true` — satisfied. backend/main.py:list_todos filters `Todo.due_date < today` when `overdue=true`; tests/test_overdue_filter.py covers past / future / null cases.
- clause: `Frontend shows the due date on each todo and has an 'overdue only' toggle` — UNSATISFIED. frontend/src/TodoItem.tsx does NOT reference due_date; grep -rn 'due_date' frontend/ returns 0 hits; no overdue toggle exists in TodoList.tsx. The frontend PR was never opened.
- clause: `Tests cover model, API, and the filter` — partially satisfied. Backend tests are in place (tests/test_api.py + tests/test_overdue_filter.py) but no frontend tests were added because there is no frontend rendering to test.

## Structural health
verdict: concerns. Backend changes are clean; frontend clause is entirely unaddressed — the shipped goal is functionally incomplete.

## Risks not in done_when
none material.

expected.notes: One clause (frontend rendering + toggle) is explicitly unsatisfied with cited evidence. Correct verdict is `off_track` with a correction naming what to add. If the model mislabels this `achieved`, `validate()`'s at-done-gate strictness must downgrade because the clauses array shows an unsatisfied entry with evidence.

canned_model_output.rationale: Backend fully implemented and green; frontend clause is missing but the model incorrectly stamped achieved anyway. This test proves validate() catches the model's mistake.

clauses.clause: Todos have an optional due_date (ISO date)
clauses.evidence: backend/models.py:14 declares due_date as a nullable Date column.
backend/main.py:list_todos filters by due_date; tests pass.

clauses.clause: Frontend shows the due date on each todo and has an 'overdue only' toggle
clauses.evidence: grep -rn due_date frontend/ returns 0 hits; TodoItem.tsx has no due_date rendering; no overdue toggle in TodoList.tsx.

clauses.clause: Tests cover model, API, and the filter
